title: quercus hirtifolia
intro: Quercus hirtifolia, also known as the hairy-leaved oak, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the beech family. The bark and leaves of this tree have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus hirtifolia are numerous and include reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Quercus hirtifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
